heading: Omitted
label: 2397a Omitted
notes: CodificationSection, , as added , , , and , (c), , , and , (c), , , was omitted in view of the reversion of this part, effective , to provisions in effect on , by , set out as a note preceding . Prior to omission, section 2397a read as follows:Pub. L. 93–618, title II, § 288Pub. L. 111–5, div. B, title I, § 1856(a)123 Stat. 394Pub. L. 112–40, title II, § 201(b)125 Stat. 403Pub. L. 114–27, title IV, § 402(b)129 Stat. 374section 406 of Pub. L. 114–27section 2271 of this titleFeb. 17, 2009Oct. 21, 2011June 29, 2015July 1, 2021Jan. 1, 2014“§ 2397a. Sense of Congress“It is the sense of Congress that the Secretaries of Labor, Commerce, and Agriculture should apply the provisions of part 2 (relating to adjustment assistance for workers), part 3 (relating to adjustment assistance for firms), part 4 (relating to adjustment assistance for communities), and part 6 (relating to adjustment assistance for farmers), respectively, with the utmost regard for the interests of workers, firms, communities, and farmers petitioning for benefits under such parts.”
